title: Digitaria aristulata
taxonomy: Digitaria aristulata is classified under the kingdom Plantae, the phylum Angiospermophyta, the class Liliopsida, the order Poales, and the family Poaceae. This classification places Digitaria aristulata within the grass family. Digitaria aristulata is further categorized within the tribe Paniceae and the genus Digitaria. The species is commonly known as aristulated finger grass or finger grass. Its classification reflects its characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Poaceae family.

intro: Digitaria aristulata is a plant species commonly known as Panicum aristulatum, a type of grass native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and digestive problems, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Digitaria aristulata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include decoctions, infusions, and tonics, which are often used to treat fever, cough, and other respiratory conditions.
